summary: Enthusiastic fans praise the show’s Help > Slipknot > Franklin’s sequence and a memorable Around & Around catcall; recording quality isn’t discussed but is presumed listenable.
review: The concert is remembered fondly for its early‑show excitement – Cold Rain & Snow, Wharf Rat and a soaring Help > Slipknot > Franklin’s – as well as the first “catcall” from Bob in Around & Around, which many cite as a Dead tradition starter.  Reviewers are uniformly upbeat and give high star ratings; no one mentions technical flaws, suggesting the tape is at least adequately clear for enjoyment.

ai_rating.rationale: Five reviews overwhelmingly positive (average ★ 4.2) and highlight several standout performances; recording quality is not criticized, so the overall rating leans toward a strong show with an acceptable source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers make no mention of sound issues, implying the audio is at least clear enough for listening; no specific details on mix or source are provided.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans note a lively first set with classic early‑Dead numbers and a particularly inspired second‑set sequence (Help > Slipknot > Franklin’s), capped by an iconic Around & Around moment that adds historical flavor.

band_member_comments.Bob: A noted “catcall” from Bob in Around and Around is highlighted as a memorable, tradition‑starting moment.
